title: Supreme Court Expands Trump’s Power Over Immigration With TPS and Southern Border Decisions
summary: The court handed President Trump victories in his push to rescind deportation protections for hundreds of thousands of people and turn away migrants at the southern border.

title: Supreme Court T.P.S. Ruling on Haiti Plunges Many Migrants Into Limbo
summary: The decision renders 1.3 million people from more than a dozen countries, many who have lived in the United States for decades, vulnerable to deportation.

title: Why the Venezuelan Earthquakes Happened, and What to Expect Next
summary: Twin earthquakes like those that ripped through the region are unusual but not unheard of. Scientists are already gathering data needed for a more detailed picture.

title: Deadly Heat Wave in Europe Continues to Smash Temperature Records
summary: The continent’s second heat wave since May continued to disrupt daily life for millions. Officials in Spain said it might have contributed to a spike in deaths.

title: Latest Victims of France’s Heat Wave: Children Locked in Family Cars
summary: As temperatures pass record levels in France, the heat wave-related death toll continues to rise.

title: See Record-Breaking Extreme Heat Wave Across Europe in Photos
summary: Temperature records have been broken in several European countries this week.

title: Trump Keeps Undercutting Republicans’ Message, Squandering His Own Trifecta
summary: Divisions between the president and his party on Capitol Hill have muddled Republicans’ midterm pitch to voters, and have crippled the G.O.P. at what should be the peak of its power.

title: What Are the Paths Forward for the Housing Bill After Trump Refused to Sign It?
summary: President Trump abruptly scrapped plans to sign a major housing bill. It could still become law, with or without a presidential signature. But Mr. Trump could also try to kill it.

title: Federal Judge Strikes Key Parts of Trump Order Restricting Mail Voting
summary: The ruling rejected the Trump administration’s attempt to change federal election procedures through an executive order.

title: Apple Raises Prices on Macs and iPads Amid the A.I. Boom
summary: The tech giant cited the soaring costs of memory and storage chips as it increased prices more than $200 on some devices.

title: IBM Unveils New Method to Make Smaller Computer Chip Parts
summary: Industry leaders had worried that innovations in chip miniaturization were no longer possible.

title: Chinese A.I. Models Gain Ground on Anthropic and OpenAI
summary: Silicon Valley engineers recently flocked to new technology from a Chinese company, Z.ai, that is almost as good as its American competitors but much cheaper.

title: Big Companies Aim to Ease A.I. Transition for American Workers
summary: OpenAI, Anthropic, Amazon and Microsoft have signed on to an effort led by Gina Raimondo, a former commerce secretary.

title: Inside the C.D.C.’s Mad Scramble to Meet Kennedy’s Demands
summary: A cache of internal emails offers a look at the pressure the nation’s public health officials faced from the new health secretary in the early months of the Trump administration.

title: Iran Threatens Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Marco Rubio Meets Gulf Leaders
summary: The new warning complicated efforts to revive use of the critical waterway as Washington sought regional support for its framework peace agreement with Iran.

title: U.S. Military’s Weapons Shortage Shows Few Signs of Easing Soon
summary: President Trump and Pentagon officials tried to reassure manufacturers as they sought additional funding from Congress.

title: This Doctor Can Change the Color of Your Eyes. Should He?
summary: Francis Ferrari, a French ophthalmologist, invented a controversial procedure that can change eye color. People from around the world are willing to take the risk.

title: Inflation Accelerated in May as Iran War Pushed Up Prices
summary: A closely watched measure of inflation ticked up as the conflict in the Middle East inflated energy prices.

title: U.S. Inflation Problems Are Far From Over
summary: The Federal Reserve’s new chairman has vowed to deliver price stability, but officials are at odds over whether that will require higher borrowing costs.

title: Why These Affordable Homes Face a 31% Rent Increase
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has promised relief for rent-stabilized tenants. But thousands of apartments under a separate city program are subject to increases, creating an awkward contrast.

summary: A freeze for rent-stabilized homes is expected when a city panel votes on Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s plan on Thursday, but the move is likely to face legal challenges.

summary: The city had accused Jasmine Ray of fiscal mismanagement. She said that she had been unfairly targeted because of her ties to the former mayor.

title: Is Space Tourism Finally Ready for Takeoff?
summary: Delays and rising prices have made the promise of space travel for ordinary people more elusive than ever. Will that ever change?

summary: Gov. Kathy Hochul has worked with Mayor Zohran Mamdani on child care and second-home taxes. But the triumphs of democratic socialists on Primary Day could complicate her vision.

title: Here’s What It Means to Be a Democratic Socialist
summary: Universal health care, taxing the wealthy and opposition to military aid to Israel are among the movement’s key tenets.

summary: After democratic socialists captured two House primaries and several state legislative contests in New York, the group’s leaders mulled how to use their expanding influence.

title: Tickled Apes Reveal the Rhythmic Roots of Laughter
summary: A study of chimps, gorillas and other great apes, including human children, sheds light on how laughter has evolved.

summary: The Supreme Court just gave the Trump Administration free rein to end Temporary Protected Status.

title: Don the Builder
summary: We look at Trump’s construction projects in Washington, D.C.
